channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Democrats slam Trump over reports he'll merge USAID with State Department
item.description: Democrats on Friday slammed President Trump over recent news reports that he is considering merging the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) with the State Department. “It is imperative that we maintain an independent development voice and capability within the U.S. government.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Serbian anti-graft protesters march to a northern city and plan to block bridges
item.description: NOVI SAD, Serbia (AP) — Hundreds of students protesting graft they blame for 15 deaths in a building collapse marched Friday through Serbia to the northern city of Novi Sad where they plan to block three Danube River bridges this weekend.
item.dc:creator: ELDAR EMRIC and DUSAN STOJANOVIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels move deeper into eastern Congo as UN reports executions and rapes
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels were quickly expanding their presence in eastern Congo after capturing Goma, the region's major city, the U.N.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump refuses to say if he’s spoken to Putin; White House ‘talking’ about ending Ukraine war
item.description: President Trump on Friday wouldn’t say if he has spoken with Russian President Vladimir Putin since he began his second term but said there are ongoing discussions about ending the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Dutch police arrest three suspects after the theft of a priceless golden helmet from Romania
item.description: Three suspects have been arrested following the theft of an ancient golden helmet from Romania that had been on display in a museum in the Netherlands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colombian president urges undocumented Colombians in US to return
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro urged undocumented Colombians who are living in the U.S. to return home, saying they would be rewarded for it. "I ask undocumented Colombians in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Trudeau says Canada 'won't back down' as Trump ups tariff threat
item.description: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Canada “won’t back down” from President Trump’s threat to impose a 25 percent tariff and that Ottawa will respond accordingly until they are removed.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: The Taliban take over Afghanistan’s only luxury hotel, more than a decade after attacking it
item.description: The Taliban are taking over the operations of Afghanistan's only luxury hotel, more than a decade after launching a deadly assault on it.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Italian court blocks asylum-seekers expulsions in challenge to Meloni’s migrant deal with Albania
item.description: An Italian appeals court has refused to approve the speedy expulsion of 43 asylum seekers detained in Albania under a controversial migration deal to move the proceedings beyond European Union borders.
item.dc:creator: Giada Zampano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's foreign aid freeze guts grassroots groups helping migrants in Latin America
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — A busy shelter for migrants in southern Mexico has been left without a doctor. A program to provide mental health support for LGBTQ+ youth fleeing Venezuela was disbanded.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, MARIA VERZA and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Venezuela frees 6 Americans after meeting between President Maduro and Trump's envoy
item.description: Six Americans who had been detained in Venezuela in recent months have freed by the government of President Nicolás Maduro after he met with a senior Trump administration official.
item.dc:creator: Regina Garcia Cano And Joshua Goodman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Grenell to give Venezuela a warning from Trump administration
item.description: President Trump’s envoy for special missions, Richard Grenell, is delivering an in-person message to Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro that there will be consequences if detained Americans are not released and Venezuelan “criminals and gang members” deported from the U.S. are not repatriated.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Lithuanian president backs Trump’s NATO defense spending goal amid ongoing Russian threat
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — Securing an end to the fighting in Ukraine must take place with Kyiv's full involvement and come with more defense spending by regional countries to avoid any future Russian aggression, according to Lithuania's president.
item.dc:creator: LIUDAS DAPKUS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump aid freeze disrupts global HIV/AIDS fight
item.description: A legacy bipartisan initiative to combat HIV and AIDS in Africa is collateral damage from President Trump’s directive to halt all U.S. foreign assistance, despite efforts to exempt humanitarian assistance and lifesaving medication from being caught up in the three-month funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli hostages' families welcome news that 3 more men will be released
item.description: The Israeli organization representing families of hostages in Gaza has celebrated the news that three high-profile men will be released next.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: German parliament expected to vote on migration bill that could pass with far-right support
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Germany's parliament is expected to vote Friday on an opposition bill calling for tougher rules on migration that could become the first legislation to pass thanks to a far-right party — adding to a controversy about the attitude of the front-runner in Germany's upcoming election towar
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A French journalist recounts 711 days of captivity as a hostage of Islamic extremists in Mali
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — For a foreign correspondent in Mali, the assignment seemed like a dream: as Islamic extremists upended the region, Olivier Dubois, a French journalist, secured a rare interview with a leader of JNIM, an al-Qaida’s affiliate in the Sahel. Or so he thought.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israelis and Palestinians rejoice after more hostages and prisoners are freed
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Eight more hostages were freed from the Gaza Strip by Hamas-led militants on Thursday in a sometimes chaotic process that briefly delayed Israel's release of 110 Palestinian prisoners and underscored the fragility of the ceasefire that began earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, WAFAA SHURAFA and ISABEL DeBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Panama's president says there will be no negotiation about ownership of canal
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Panama President José Raúl Mulino said Thursday there will be no negotiation with the United States over ownership of the Panama Canal, and he hopes U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's ban on UN's Palestinian aid agency has come into effect. Here's what that looks like
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel on Thursday formally banned the main United Nations aid agency for Palestinian refugees from operating on its territory despite heavy diplomatic backlash, a move that humanitarian officials warn could have a disastrous impact on aid delivery and jeopardize regional stability
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk nominated for Nobel Peace Prize by European Parliament member
item.description: A European Parliament member from Slovenia says he nominated tech giant Elon Musk for a Nobel Peace Prize for his free speech advocacy. “The proposal that Mr.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Senate Democrats revolt against Trump in fallout from foreign aid freeze
item.description: Senate Democrats are starting to revolt against President Trump’s agenda on Capitol Hill amid fallout from an initial freeze on all foreign assistance, warning Republicans that failure to work together will incur consequences. Sen. Chris Coons (D-Del.) on Thursday reversed support for Rep.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Gisèle Pelicot's ex-husband, imprisoned for raping and drugging her, now caught up in other cases
item.description: NANTERRE, France (AP) — Dominique Pélicot, the convicted rapist who horrified France by drugging his then wife so other men could rape her, was questioned Thursday about other cases of rape and murder that he's suspected in.
item.dc:creator: NICOLAS GARRIGA and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France hands over last military base in Chad
item.description: N’DJAMENA, Chad (AP) — France handed over its last military base in Chad to local authorities on Thursday, two months after the former French colony in central Africa broke off its defense cooperation agreement with Paris.
item.dc:creator: MOUTA ALI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu furious about chaotic handover of Israeli hostages from Hamas
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu ordered a delay in the planned release of terrorists, seeking assurance regarding the release of hostages, but the prime minister's office later indicated that the demand had been satisfied.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu delays Palestinian prisoner release after 'chaotic' Hamas scenes
item.description: Israel will delay the release of Palestinian prisoners in Israeli jails amid chaotic scenes surrounding Hamas's release of hostages it kidnapped from Israel and held since its attack on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Chaos and uncertainty swirl around Trump's foreign aid freeze
item.description: President Trump’s decision to clean house at the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) and initially freeze all foreign assistance is fueling chaos and uncertainty in Washington and across the world.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Hostage releases begin in Gaza Strip
item.description: Hamas handed captive Israeli soldier Agam Berger over to the Red Cross, the first of eight hostages set to be released today.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former Hamas hostage Naama Levy breaks silence in first message since her release
item.description: Naama Levy, one of the four IDF soldiers released from Hamas captivity on Saturday, writes a message of gratitude in her first statement since arriving home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US consulate warns of gun battles, IEDs, kidnappings in Mexican border towns near Texas
item.description: U.S. officials have put several Mexican border towns near Texas under the highest-level travel advisory amid gun battles, kidnappings and IEDs in the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LIVE: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Holocaust survivor recalls mother's encounter with Nazi
item.description: Marianne Muller joins “NewsNation Prime” to share her family's story of courage and survival during the Holocaust.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: A child's life in Gaza, as explained by a Middle East reporter
item.description: Middle East reporter Arwa Damon, who founded the nonprofit INARA, described what she's seen in Gaza to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: CIA believes COVID most likely originated from lab but has low confidence in its own finding
item.description: The CIA now believes the virus responsible for the COVID-19 pandemic most likely originated from a laboratory, according to an assessment released Saturday that points the finger at China even while acknowledging that the spy agency has “low confidence” in its own conclusion.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, WFLA, NBC
